questions.question: Lean and clean manufacturing objectives are:
options: to eliminate or reduce those activities that do not add value to a product.
to increase the number of material used
to increase the number of packages for a product.
to eliminate or reduce the environmental impact.
correct_answers: to eliminate or reduce those activities that do not add value to a product.
to eliminate or reduce the environmental impact.

questions.question: Which of the following is / are example(s) of clean technologies?
Sustainability requires a balance between:
Sustainability assurance is:
A green product is:
options: a product designed to increase its environmental impact.
a product designed to reduce its environmental impact.
correct_answers: a product designed to reduce its environmental impact.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is / are true?
options: Utilization of non-renewable materials must increase to reduce waste.
The energy consumed per unit of product, according to the principles of sustainability, can be increased if it comes from renewable sources.
Clean manufacturing ensures responsible use of energy.
In lean manufacturing there is overproduction.
correct_answers: Clean manufacturing ensures responsible use of energy.

questions.question: The industrial ecology focuses on:
options: identifying inefficiencies and losses in manufacturing processes
correct_answers: identifying inefficiencies and losses in manufacturing processes

questions.question: Partnerships and alliances are external challenges for a company.
The results of a production process are not only finite products.
The increase of flexibility in a manufacturing system, involves, usually, additional cost.
The anticipation stock is not a safety stock.
The smaller the space-manufacturing ratio, the more efficient the manufacturing system is.
The production is smaller if a machine availability is larger.
CAD increases design time.
CAE is used in various systems simulation.
CAM uses the computer for several processes.
CAPP decreases the process planning time.
Quality assessment is more effective if done during the production cycle.
Production control involves also the inventory control.
Production planning does not involve the labor planning.
Databases are the binding agents between different departments of a company.
In any production system the human resource is present.
A rocket requires a job shop-type manufacturing system.
Bread production requires a job shop-type manufacturing system.
A car requires a line manufacturing system.
In a two workstation manufacturing system, the routings may be fixed or variable.
In a production system with more than two workstations, the routings can be fixed or variable.
A flexible manufacturing system is a system with one automated workstation with variable routings.
A flexible manufacturing system is a system with multiple automated workstation with fixed routings.
The learning curve occurs when the time to perform a given operation decreases with the number of repetitions of the same operation.
A vertically integrated plant only assembles the products.
The complexity of a product is given by the number of components or the number of operations needed for manufacturing.
The operation cycle time is the actual processing time.
Production capacity increases when the production rate increases.
For quantity type mass production, in the calculation of the operation cycle time, the system setup time must be included.
For the flow line mass production, the maximum operation time of all stations on the line is included in the calculation of the cycle time.
Utilization of a machine is not equivalent with the availability of a machine.
Work-in-process are the components or products ready for delivery.
Assemble to order is applied for the production of a car.
Make to order is applied when the demand can't be predicted.
The costs associated with material handling must be as large as possible.
The design of a material handling system depends on the type of manufacturing system where it is embedded.
For moving the large and heavy components, autonomous guided vehicles are recommended.
The "flow diagram" is a graphical method used to analyse transportation systems.
The cycle time for delivery, in the case of transport systems based on vehicles, depends on the speed of vehicles.
The number of vehicles required into a transport system depends on the vehicles availability.
Recirculating conveyors are not the most efficient transport systems.
In the case of a conveyor-type transport system, the products should be distributed evenly.
Availability of ASRS is a measure of its reliability.
In the case of a carousel storage system, its robot arm moves to each basket.
An ASRS is more efficient when performing in single operation mode than in dual operation mode.
The group technology can not be used for production systems that produce in batches.
The group technology applies only if the products can be grouped.
isual inspection is the cheapest method used to classify products.
Product classification systems may not use the design features.
In the case of polycode the interpretation of each symbol from the sequence depends on the previous symbol.
For the chain-type structure the interpretation of each symbol in the sequence is always the same.
Monocode encodes more information.
Sorting of process routings is a step in the production flow analysis.
Increased flexibility leads to higher costs.
Stock levels decreases by using an FMS.
In an FMS occurrence of errors should not stop completely the production.
A flexible manufacturing cell has minimum four machines and a materials handling system.
A dedicated FMS is less useful than an universal one.
Human resource is not a component of an FMS.
Designing an FMS requires information about the physical characteristics of the products.
Discrete event simulation offers the most accurate method for modeling an FMS.
Processes Planning requires information on existing tools and fasteners.
PP requires information about manufacturing processes and experience to achieve a consistent planning.
The assembling method of a product depends on the expected throughput.
Use of a computer in planning processes decreases the time required to develop the process plan.
Retrieval CAPP systems use the principles of group technology.
Generative CAPP systems use artificial intelligence algorithms.
Using concurrent engineering concepts, the lead time decreases.
Design for manufacturing and assembly involves changes at the organizational level in a company.
Manufacturing research involves the optimization of production processes.
The constant production more often creates large stocks.
In the case of the variable production, labor demand remains constant if you can vary the number of hours worked.
Master production schedule is generally developed in the medium term.
Independent demand is the one in which demand for a product does not depend on the demand for another product.
Master production schedule must consider the information related to capabilities.
Capacity planning deals with the allocation of equipment.
Production planning involves planning labor and equipment required.
Making a production plan involves the exclusive use of a production strategy according with the demand.
In the case of level production strategy, the number of employees and the hours worked are variable.
The order release module may contain the list of necessary raw materials.
Order scheduling module provides information about the priority order of operations.
Shortest operation next rule maximize the number of processed orders.
Order slack time rule addresses the sequencing of jobs on the basis of the value of work remaining.
When earliest due date rule is used, the highest priority is assigned to the part with the latest due date.
The order progress module generates progress reports.
The factory data collection system communicates with the control system.
In order to restore stocks at certain points in time it is necessary to know the quantity to order.
Operations planning is not for the manufacturing resource planner.
Sustainable development compromises ensuring resources for the next generation.
Sustainability assurance is a destination for any company.
In sustainable manufacturing focus is on making products using fewer materials and energy.
The sustainable manufacturing involves the removal of waste from the manufacturing process.
Green technologies provide pollution prevention and control.
A green product eliminates its environmental impact.
Environmental impact of a product should be considered from the design.
The products that minimize the use of packaging can be considered green.
Optimization of production is a measure required to ensure sustainability in manufacturing.
Sustainable manufacturing is based on reducing pollution.
In lean manufacturing there is no overproduction.
Clean manufacturing ensures responsible use of energy.
Substitution of raw materials with more polluting ones is a method of implementing clean manufacturing.
Lean and Clean manufacturing provides support for the viability of a business.
Donating equipment is not a practice encouraged by the principles of sustainability.
Implementing the principles of sustainability rests solely to the management team of a company.
Utilization of reusable materials / recycled materials must be increased.
The energy consumed per unit of product, according to the principles of sustainability can be increased if it comes from renewable sources.
Utilization of non-renewable materials must increase to reduce waste.
A product must contain as many recycled or reusable components.
The ultra modern manufacturing involves the use of cyber-physical systems.
The ultra modern manufacturing does not involve the use of IoT.
A very large amount of data is acquired and processed in an ultra modern manufacturing system.
The Industry 4.0 has emerged with the first PLCs and computers.
The cyber-physical systems are related to the third industrial revolution.
The user of the cloud computing services has to know the physical location and configuration of equipment that provides these services.
Information technology is used in quality control.
The digital factory uses information technology in all its activities.
Predictive manufacturing systems require a large amount of data collected from sensors.
The embedded systems encapsulate the cyber-physical systems.
In IoT, all the products are interconnected through the Internet.
IIoT has lower requirements than those specific IoT.
The cyber-physical systems are used in smart manufacturing.
The control system of a traditional factory is hierarchic.
The decisions are context aware in smart factories.
Industry 4.0 uses cyber physical systems.
Industry 4.0 creates intelligent manufacturing systems.
Interoperability is high in Industry 4.0.
Romania is not a target for implementation of Industry 4.0.
Equipment used in factories that implement Industry 4.0 concepts have to be very reliable.
